title: BadmintonForehandClearRAGChunk
description: Concept-level chunk for the badminton forehand-clear RAG knowledge base. evidence_level here is the CHUNK-level coarse evidence layer, decoupled from the SOURCE-level fine-grained label in badminton_forehand_clear_sources.csv. See the mapping table in the knowledge base §8.2.

chunk_id.description: Stable unique id in snake_case, e.g., bh_clear_phase_p3_forward_swing_001

text.description: Chunk body in Chinese. Cite source ids like [S05]. Tag analogy sources inline, e.g., [S11^smash] / [S17^msk], so the analogy boundary survives chunk-level retrieval.

source_ids.description: References source.id in the source catalog. Pattern allows S100+ as the corpus grows.

phase.description: Stage key bound to the P0-P5 model in §3.2. Mapping: whole_stroke=整套动作; movement_to_rearcourt=P0; preparation=P1; backswing_loading=P2; forward_swing_acceleration=P3; impact=P4; follow_through_recovery=P5; validation/rag_instruction/source_note=非阶段类 chunk.

phase_id.description: P0-P5 stage id from §3.2; NA for non-stage chunks (definition/metric/implementation/source_note).

body_region.description: May be empty [] for non-action chunks (definition/implementation/source_note).

joint_dof.description: May be empty [] for non-action chunks.

muscle_groups.description: Muscle GROUP names only; never finger intrinsic/extrinsic muscles (boundary §0). May be empty [].

excluded_muscle_groups.description: Muscles explicitly excluded by the 'no hand/fingers' boundary, e.g., flexor_digitorum_superficialis, flexor_digitorum_profundus, extensor_digitorum, hand_intrinsics.

metrics.description: May be empty [] for non-metric chunks.

evidence_level.description: CHUNK-level coarse evidence layer. Maps to KB §8.1 priority groups A-E plus method/implementation. Distinct from source.evidence_level.

limits_of_evidence.description: Required. State direct-vs-analogy boundary and what must NOT be over-claimed. Use '' only for pure implementation chunks.

content_hash.description: sha256 of normalized text, for reproducible eval/version pinning.
